name: FEMOROACETABULAR IMPINGEMENT
definition: repetitive microtrauma due to an anatomic conflict between proximal femur + acetabular rim

demographics.Mean age: 20-45 yrs; in patients with increased physical activity; M÷F = 3÷2 to 9÷1

dominant_finding: aspheric (= osseous bump) femoral head / head-neck junction (50%)
discriminating_features: aspheric (= osseous bump) femoral head / head-neck junction (50%)
broad areas of cartilage lesions often > 1 cm in width
α-angle of > 55° (measured at the anterosuperior position on radial images rotated

general: aspheric (= osseous bump) femoral head / head-neck junction (50%)
broad areas of cartilage lesions often > 1 cm in width
α-angle of > 55° (measured at the anterosuperior position on radial images rotated
deep acetabulum (head center > 5 mm below rim)
acetabular retroversion = anterior acetabular rim overlaps posterior rim on AP
osseous bump deforming femoral head-neck junction (33%)
thin rim of adjacent cartilage lesion often < 5 mm in width
cystic / bony proliferative changes at femoral head-neck junction
ossicle along acetabular rim / os acetabuli

substantial overlap in α-angle measurements between volunteers and patients
clinical.Cx: premature osteoarthritis initially with cartilage damage + labral tears
clinical.Rx: surgery in patients without osteoarthritis consisting of reshaping of femoral waist /
